summary: Iteration 32 — Verified the Work Order send-to-sub Gmail dispatch fix in /app/backend/work_orders.py. Created /app/backend/tests/test_work_order_send_email.py with 4 pytest cases covering draft, send (basic + with library files), and preview-with-newlines. ALL 4 PASS. Critical assertion `email_sent=true` is now satisfied — backend log shows: `WO email sent to darren@darrenoliverllc.com (2 attachments)` for the basic send and `(4 attachments)` when 2 library files are added. The previous SMTP_* env-var lookup bug is fixed: _send_email() now wraps email_sender.send_email() using GMAIL_USERNAME/GMAIL_APP_PASSWORD.

verified_flows.Backend log confirmation: work_orders.py logged 'WO email sent to darren@darrenoliverllc.com (2 attachments)' then '(4 attachments)' — Gmail SMTP actually dispatched

critical_code_review_comments: _send_email() now properly translates {bytes,filename,mime} → email_sender's {data,filename,mime} shape and derives a plain-text fallback via regex tag strip. Clean wrapper. ✅

Line 769 in work_orders.py still references `os.environ.get('SMTP_FROM')` as a fallback rep email when the deal has no assigned_user_id. That env var does not exist in /app/backend/.env — should be GMAIL_FROM_EMAIL. Minor bug (only hits the rep-notify path on the public sign endpoint when a deal has no assigned user); not exercised by this test but worth a one-line follow-up fix.
EmailNotConfigured exception is caught and logged with `log.error` — good; failure mode is non-fatal to the API caller (returns email_sent=false rather than 500).

description newline handling at line 209 (raw_desc.replace(' ',' ').replace(' ','
')) correctly converts both and into
tags so ReportLab Paragraph renders blank lines.
action_items: (Optional, low priority) Replace `os.environ.get('SMTP_FROM')` on line 769 of /app/backend/work_orders.py with `os.environ.get('GMAIL_FROM_EMAIL')` so the rep-notify email after sub e-signs still has a valid fallback recipient when a deal lacks assigned_user_id.
seed_data_creation: None — only the TEST_Lead Deal (640a9104-…) was touched, exactly as instructed. One work_orders row upserted for that deal (idempotent — re-running the test replaces, never duplicates).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Work Order send→Gmail flow is fully verified end-to-end. Real emails were dispatched to darren@darrenoliverllc.com during this test run (2 of them — one with WO+Spec, one with WO+Spec+2 library PDFs). pytest_iter32.xml is the regression artifact. Only nit found: line 769 still references SMTP_FROM env var (dead fallback) in the public-sign rep-notify path — not blocking, easy one-line fix.
rca_of_the_issue: Original bug: work_orders._send_email() used a hand-rolled smtplib helper that read SMTP_HOST/SMTP_USER/SMTP_PASSWORD/SMTP_FROM which are NOT defined in /app/backend/.env (only GMAIL_USERNAME/GMAIL_APP_PASSWORD/GMAIL_FROM_EMAIL are). Result: helper threw inside the try block and returned False — API responded with email_sent=false and the sub never received the email. Fix: _send_email() now imports email_sender.send_email() (the same helper proposals/invoices use), translates the attachment dict shape, and forwards. Verified by both pytest assertions and backend INFO log line `WO email sent to darren@darrenoliverllc.com (N attachments)`.
